gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Bicuspid aortic valve (BAV) is a heritable congenital heart defect with unknown genetic origins. Previous studies (linkage, candidate sequencing, small GWAS) failed to identify robust genetic associations. The goal was to discover genetic variants associated with BAV using a GWAS approach enriched for protein-altering variants, followed by replication and functional validation.

section_gists.heading: Protein-altering and regulatory genetic variants near GATA4 implicated in bicuspid aortic valve
section_gists.gist: Introduction: BAV is the most common cardiovascular malformation (~1% prevalence), moderately heritable (20-89%), and associated with serious complications (aortic stenosis, dissection). Previous genetic studies (linkage, candidate sequencing, one small GWAS with 68 cases) failed to identify significant loci except NOTCH1 in <6% of cases. The study aims to perform a larger GWAS with protein-altering variant enrichment to discover new BAV-associated variants.
process_signals: Prior GWAS with 68 cases found no genome-wide significant results, motivating larger sample size.
Hypothesis: BAV has a complex genetic basis with multiple variants of incomplete penetrance.
Focus on protein-altering variants due to enrichment on genotyping array.
methods: Genotyping array: Illumina Human CoreExome (498,075 variants, 43.8% protein-altering)
Replication in up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls
failures_or_limits: Previous GWAS (n=68) underpowered, no significant findings.
NOTCH1 variants explain only a small fraction of BAV.

section_gists.heading: Results
section_gists.gist: GWAS discovery identified a low-frequency intergenic variant rs6601627 (MAF=4.1%, OR=1.9, P=3.0e-15) 151 kb from GATA4, and a common missense variant p.Ser377Gly in GATA4 (rs3729856, MAF=14.5%, OR=1.31, P=8.8e-8 after replication). The two variants are independent (LD r2=0.013). Functional follow-up: chromatin conformation (Hi-C, ChIA-PET) showed looping between the noncoding variant region and GATA4 introns; luciferase assays suggested enhancer activity; GATA4 disruption in iPSC-derived endothelial cells impaired endothelial-to-mesenchymal transition (EndoMT), a key process in valve development. A rare missense variant in DHX38 (p.T1221M) also reached genome-wide significance but was not further studied.
process_signals: Discovery of genome-wide significant noncoding variant near GATA4 led to hypothesis that GATA4 is the causal gene.
Missense variant in GATA4 selected for replication due to proximity and protein-altering nature.
Conditional analysis confirmed independence of the two GATA4 variants.
Functional experiments designed to test regulatory mechanism of noncoding variant and role of GATA4 in valve development.
EndoMT impairment by GATA4 disruption supports its role in BAV.
methods: GWAS discovery: logistic regression with age, sex, PCs as covariates
Replication: in silico in additional cohorts
Chromatin conformation: Hi-C and ChIA-PET data from ENCODE
Luciferase reporter assays for enhancer activity
CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of GATA4 in iPSCs, differentiation to endothelial cells, EndoMT assay (collagen gel)
metrics: LD r2=0.013 between the two variants
ExAC: GATA4 pLI=0.8, missense variant predicted benign/tolerated
EndoMT: GATA4 sgRNA reduced mesenchymal cells to ~30% of control
failures_or_limits: No significant eQTL detected for rs6601627 in GTEx, suggesting effect may be developmental or modest.
Missense variant rs3729856 predicted benign/tolerated by PolyPhen-2 and SIFT, CADD score 9.418 (top 11% deleterious).
Functional mechanism of noncoding variant not fully established; only suggestive chromatin looping and enhancer activity.

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: The study identifies genome-wide significant variants near GATA4 associated with BAV. The noncoding variant may affect GATA4 expression via chromatin looping, potentially disrupting EndoMT. GATA4 is a known cardiac transcription factor; mutations cause various congenital heart defects. The missense variant p.Ser377Gly also associates with tetralogy of Fallot (ToF), suggesting pleiotropy. The noncoding variant is specific to BAV. Limitations include incomplete functional validation, European-only discovery cohort, and modest sample size for rare variants.
process_signals: Hypothesis: noncoding variant disrupts a regulatory element critical for GATA4 expression during valve development.
Missense variant may have broader cardiac effects (associated with ToF).
Acknowledged need for future functional experiments to confirm mechanism.
Population-specific variant frequencies noted (e.g., noncoding variant rare in East Asians).
methods: Look-up of GATA4 variants in ToF cases
Comparison of variant frequencies across populations (1000 Genomes)
metrics: p.Ser377Gly associated with ToF (not quantified in text)
Noncoding variant rs6601627: 108/115 correlated variants have MAF<0.01 in East Asians
failures_or_limits: Cannot confirm mechanism of noncoding variant; only suggestive chromatin interaction.
Functional experiments not performed in developing heart tissue.
Discovery cohort limited to European ancestry; generalizability uncertain.
Sample size limits power for rare variant discovery.
Incomplete penetrance and complex genetics acknowledged.

section_gists.heading: Methods
section_gists.gist: Detailed methods: GWAS genotyping on Illumina Human CoreExome array, imputation to HRC panel, QC (sample and variant filters), case-control matching (age, sex, ethnicity), association testing with logistic regression. Replication in multiple cohorts. Functional assays: chromatin conformation data from ENCODE, luciferase assays, iPSC generation and CRISPR-Cas9 disruption of GATA4, endothelial differentiation, EndoMT assay.
process_signals: Stringent QC to avoid batch effects (same array, same center).
Greedy matching algorithm for controls to maximize ancestry matching and power for rare variants.
Use of publicly available functional genomics data (GTEx, ENCODE) to prioritize GATA4.
iPSC and CRISPR methods to test GATA4 function in vitro.
methods: Imputation: SHAPEIT2 phasing, minimac3 imputation to HRC r1.1
QC: sample call rate >98%, variant call rate >98%, HWE P>1e-5, cluster separation >0.3, imputation r2>0.3
Association: logistic regression (EPACTS, PLINK) with age, sex, 4 PCs
Replication: IMPUTE2 imputation, SNPTEST logistic regression
Functional: Hi-C, ChIA-PET, ChromHMM, luciferase, iPSC-CRISPR, EndoMT
failures_or_limits: Potential for residual confounding despite matching.
Functional assays in cell lines may not fully recapitulate in vivo development.
No direct evidence of variant effect on GATA4 expression in developing heart.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: Initial hypothesis: BAV has a complex genetic architecture with multiple common and rare variants.
GWAS discovery of noncoding variant near GATA4 shifted focus to GATA4 as causal gene.
Missense variant in GATA4 provided additional support, though predicted benign.
Functional experiments aimed to link noncoding variant to GATA4 regulation and GATA4 to valve development via EndoMT.
Pleiotropy of missense variant (BAV and ToF) suggests different mechanisms for coding and noncoding variants.
uncertainties_to_resolve: Exact causal variant(s) and mechanism by which noncoding region affects GATA4 expression.
Whether the noncoding variant acts in developing heart valves in vivo.
Functional significance of the missense variant p.Ser377Gly despite benign predictions.
Contribution of other loci (e.g., DHX38) to BAV.

evidence_bank.quote: Despite the prevalence, importance and heritability of BAV, its genetic origins remain elusive.
Only one previous genome-wide association study (GWAS) for BAV has been published in a limited number of cases (n=68; ref. 14), which did not identify any genome-wide significant results.
here we perform an unbiased genome scan in a large study of BAV cases (n=466) and controls (n=4,660), with replication in additional samples of up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls.

We propose that this noncoding variant, or a variant tagged by it, influences GATA4 expression in a manner not detectable by GTE—either exerting an influence on gene expression levels only in the developing fetal heart or with a relatively modest effect that was not detectable in the current GTEx sample size.

event_graph.paper_main_problem: Bicuspid aortic valve (BAV) is a heritable congenital heart defect with unknown genetic origins. Previous studies (linkage, candidate sequencing, small GWAS) failed to identify robust genetic associations. The goal was to discover genetic variants associated with BAV using a GWAS approach enriched for protein-altering variants, followed by replication and functional validation.

canonical_events.summary: BAV is heritable but genetic origins remain elusive; previous GWAS underpowered (n=68) with no significant findings.
Study design: GWAS with 466 cases and 4,660 controls, replication in up to 1,326 cases and 8,103 controls, using Illumina Human CoreExome array enriched for protein-altering variants.

GWAS discovery identified a low-frequency intergenic variant rs6601627 (MAF=4.1%, OR=1.93, P=3.0e-15) 151 kb from GATA4, and a common missense variant p.Ser377Gly in GATA4 (rs3729856, MAF=14.5%, OR=1.31, P=3.2e-6 in discovery).
Replication in multiple cohorts confirmed association of rs6601627 and rs3729856; rs3729856 reached near genome-wide significance after replication (P=8.8e-8).
Identified rs118065347 as a likely functional variant co-localizing with multiple transcription factor binding sites and in a known enhancer active in fetal heart.
Chromatin interaction loops from ChIA-PET and Hi-C data show that the region containing rs118065347 loops to an enhancer near GATA4, suggesting potential regulatory interaction.
